methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA to boost community AI education signals deliberate effort to build distributed, deep capability beyond elite tech corridors—directly challenging the concentration dynamic if peer learning networks activate across Richmond.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates intentional capacity-building in historically underserved Appalachian regions, testing whether distributed deep learning can reach beyond metro hubs into coal-transition communities.

signals.title: Fortreum acquires Reston-based cybersecurity firm
signals.signal_reading: Fortreum's acquisition of Reston AI compliance startup Kovr.ai reinforces NoVA's concentration of specialized AI expertise and capital flows, deepening the capability gap as sophisticated AI knowledge clusters in the DC-adjacent corridor.

signals.title: 6,000-acre innovation district launches in Manassas
signals.signal_reading: The 6,000-acre Manassas innovation district linking George Mason University with regional infrastructure creates institutional architecture for concentrated deep capability, potentially widening the gap unless knowledge transfer mechanisms extend beyond Prince William County.

signals.title: Doug Hoven on How AI Is Changing the Way Homes Are Bought and Sold in Hampton Roads - The Ritz Herald
signals.signal_reading: AI platform adoption in Hampton Roads real estate represents shallow, tool-based distribution—users consuming AI capabilities without building underlying technical depth, signaling broad but surface-level penetration in non-tech sectors.
